evals.prompt:
Build the navigation README backbone for a topic from its MIF findings and confirm the structure. Run exactly:

  OUTDIR=$(pwd)
  bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/build-topic-readme.sh example-okf-mif-knowledge-spine --findings $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/findings --out $OUTDIR/README.md

Confirm the README has the verdict-aware metadata header and every required section.
evals.expected_output: README.md with an H1 title; a metadata header reading '**Findings:** 3 (survived 3) | **Sources:** 2 unique URLs' and a Status line; and Purpose, Dimensions, Key Findings, Reports, Findings by Dimension, and Tags sections. Counts and the verdict breakdown are projected from the substrate, not hand-authored.

deterministic_checks.description: verdict-aware counts + exact source total, projected from the substrate
the per-dimension findings table is present
the Key Findings section is present

expectations: The README is a navigation index, not a MIF report — H1 title, no MIF frontmatter.
The metadata header carries the verdict breakdown and the exact unique-source count.
All structural sections are present, including Findings by Dimension.

evals.prompt:
The gate must fail closed when a README is missing. Run:

  OUTDIR=$(pwd)
  bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/build-topic-readme.sh example-okf-mif-knowledge-spine --out $OUTDIR/none.md --check ; echo "check exit: $?"

The check must exit non-zero (the README does not exist).
evals.expected_output: build-topic-readme.sh --check exits non-zero because the README does not exist; the gate fails closed.

deterministic_checks.description: the validation gate exits non-zero for a missing README (fails closed)

expectations: The check is a real gate, not advisory — a missing README returns non-zero.
The agent reports the failure honestly rather than claiming the README shipped.

evals.prompt:
A just-created topic with zero findings must still get a valid README (the 'created' trigger). Run:

  OUTDIR=$(pwd)
  mkdir -p $OUTDIR/empty-findings
  bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/build-topic-readme.sh example-okf-mif-knowledge-spine --findings $OUTDIR/empty-findings --out $OUTDIR/README.md
  bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/build-topic-readme.sh example-okf-mif-knowledge-spine --findings $OUTDIR/empty-findings --out $OUTDIR/README.md --check

Confirm the zero-findings README is valid and passes the check (the synthesis gate is exempt — there is nothing to synthesize).
evals.expected_output: A README with Findings: 0, a 'No findings yet' Key Findings bullet, all required sections; --check passes.

deterministic_checks.description: a created-but-unstarted topic reports zero findings/sources
empty state is handled without erroring

deterministic_checks.command: mkdir -p empty-findings && bash "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/build-topic-readme.sh" example-okf-mif-knowledge-spine --findings empty-findings --out README.md && bash "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/build-topic-readme.sh" example-okf-mif-knowledge-spine --findings empty-findings --out README.md --check
deterministic_checks.description: the zero-findings README builds and passes the gate (synthesis check exempt)

expectations: The 'created' trigger produces a valid README even before any research has run.
Empty/partial state never errors the build or the check.

evals.prompt: The gate ENFORCES synthesis: a freshly built skeleton (Key Findings = the deterministic draft) must be refused, and the same README must pass once Key Findings are synthesized. Demonstrate the full cycle.
evals.expected_output: The un-synthesized skeleton fails --check with a 'synthesis not applied' error; after the Key Findings are rewritten as cross-finding bullets, --check passes. This is the floor that prevents shipping the skeleton.

deterministic_checks.command: set -e; SF="$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/findings"; bash "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/build-topic-readme.sh" example-okf-mif-knowledge-spine --findings "$SF" --out README.md; if bash "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/build-topic-readme.sh" example-okf-mif-knowledge-spine --findings "$SF" --out README.md --check 2>/dev/null; then echo 'skeleton wrongly passed'; exit 1; fi; awk '/^## Key Findings$/{print;print "";print "- Synthesized cross-finding bullet with specifics.";print "";s=1;next} s&&/^## /{s=0;print;next} s{next} {print}' README.md > R && mv R README.md; bash "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/build-topic-readme.sh" example-okf-mif-knowledge-spine --findings "$SF" --out README.md --check
deterministic_checks.description: skeleton is refused (synthesis not applied); synthesized README passes the gate

expectations: The gate distinguishes the auto-generated draft from real synthesis and refuses the draft.
Once Key Findings synthesize across findings, the same README passes.
This guarantees the shipped README is never the bare skeleton.
